title: 10 best herbal tinctures for stomach discomfort
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for stomach discomfort are concentrated liquid extracts derived from plants, used to alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and digestive issues. These natural remedies help soothe the stomach by calming inflammation, regulating digestion, and relaxing the muscles. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with stomach discomfort include Peppermint, Ginger, and Fennel, which can provide quick relief from nausea and indigestion. By using these tinctures, individuals can enjoy improved digestion, reduced discomfort, and a better overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with stomach discomfort because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the stomach lining. The bioactive compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, work to calm stomach upset, alleviate nausea, and ease cramps, making it a popular natural remedy for indigestion, bloating, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
remedy_properties: Digestive enzyme stimulation: Ginger's tincture helps stimulate digestive enzymes to break down food more efficiently, reducing bloating and discomfort caused by poor digestion.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), acid reflux, and other gastrointestinal disorders.
Gastric motility regulation: Ginger's tincture helps regulate the movement of food through the digestive system, preventing stagnation and reducing symptoms of constipation, nausea, and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of ginger have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate digestive issues and relieve stomach discomfort.
Roots: Ginger roots are rich in compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have natural carminative and anti-nausea effects, making them effective for soothing stomach upset.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh ginger roots from a trusted source, selecting those with minimal mold or damage.
Wash the ginger roots thoroughly with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the cleaned ginger roots into small pieces, measuring out 250-300 grams for this recipe.
Combine the chopped ginger with 70% proof vodka in a glass jar, filling it to the top and ensuring all ginger is submerged.
Store the jar in a cool dark place, allowing it to steep for 6-8 weeks before straining and filtering the liquid into small bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with stomach discomfort because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the digestive system. The menthol in peppermint relaxes the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing cramps and spasms that can cause discomfort. Additionally, peppermint's antimicrobial effects help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the gut, allowing for smoother digestion and relief from symptoms such as bloating, gas, and indigestion. This natural remedy provides quick and effective relief for those experiencing stomach upset.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint tincture contains compounds like menthol and methanol, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms such as bloating and cramping.
Digestive soothing properties: The menthol in peppermint tincture helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause discomfort, nausea, and vomiting.

Antispasmodic properties: Peppermint tincture's ability to calm muscle spasms in the stomach and intestines can help alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain, diarrhea, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a primary source of menthol and other bioactive compounds that help alleviate stomach discomfort by reducing inflammation and spasms.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing digestive issues like cramps and indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality peppermint leaves of mentha x piperita in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Chop fresh peppermint leaves into small pieces and pack them tightly into clean glass jars leaving about one inch at the top for liquid expansion.
Pour 80 proof vodka or other neutral spirit over the packed peppermint leaves to create a 1:2 ratio of herb to solvent.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a dark, cool place for four weeks allowing the mixture to steep and extract.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and labeling with date and ingredients used.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with stomach discomfort because of its unique ability to soothe and calm the digestive system. The herb glycyrrhizin, present in licorice root, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining, alleviating symptoms such as heartburn, bloating, and cramps. Additionally, licorice tincture can also increase mucus production, which helps protect and heal the stomach lining, promoting a healthy gut environment and reducing discomfort.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms such as heartburn, bloating, and abdominal pain.

Licoric acid: This component has been shown to have antacid properties, which can help neutralize stomach acid and provide relief from acidity and discomfort associated with stomach ulcers and gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD).

Flavonoids and polyphenols: These compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the digestive tract from damage caused by free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, leading to improved digestion and reduced symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are often used in medicinal tinctures due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Rhyzomes: Licorice rhyzomes contain a high amount of glycerrhetinic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a reputable supplier.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered root with 200 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another container and discard the solids after 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with stomach discomfort because it contains bioactive compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory and antacid properties. The tincture's bitter molecules stimulate digestive enzymes, improving digestion and absorption of nutrients, which can alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea. Additionally, dandelion's bitters also help to soothe the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by acidic or spicy foods, and promoting a healthy gut microbiome.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Dandelion tincture's antioxidant properties help protect the digestive system from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to stomach discomfort.

Choleretic action: Dandelion tincture stimulates the production of bile, which helps to improve digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and gas by facilitating the breakdown and absorption of nutrients.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The bioactive compounds in dandelion tincture, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe and calm irritated stomach lining, reducing discomfort and inflammation associated with conditions like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that can help soothe and calm digestive issues.
Fruits (achenes): Dandelion fruits have been traditionally used to treat stomach complaints due to their ability to stimulate digestion and relieve indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in quantities of 500 grams per liter of solvent.
Chop the herb into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction and combine with high-proof vodka at a ratio of 1:5.
Steep the mixture in a clean glass jar for 6-8 weeks, shaking daily to ensure uniform extraction, under ambient room temperature.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another container, discarding solids and retaining the clear tincture, after 60 days of maceration.
Store the filtered tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to one year, ensuring proper labeling and storage conditions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with stomach discomfort because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. The active compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to soothe and calm irritated stomach lining, alleviating symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and gas. Additionally, turmeric's natural antimicrobial properties help to eliminate harmful bacteria and yeast that can cause discomfort and imbalance in the gut. By promoting a healthy digestive environment, turmeric tinctures can provide fast and effective relief from stomach upset.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Turmeric's active compound, curcuminoids, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea.

Bioactive compounds: Turmeric tinctures contain a range of bioactive compounds, including sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids, that exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe gastrointestinal issues like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD).

Gastric soothing agents: Turmeric's saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids have a direct soothing effect on the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with gastritis, ulcers, and acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes contain a high concentration of curcuminoids, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing stomach discomfort.
Roots: Turmeric roots have been used traditionally to treat digestive issues, including nausea, bloating, and diarrhea, due to their ability to stimulate digestion and relieve gut inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric roots or purchase high-quality dried powder from reputable sources.
Grind the turmeric roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 10 grams of turmeric powder with 100ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks shaking the jar daily to allow extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean glass container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with stomach discomfort because it contains a unique combination of compounds that work together to soothe and calm the digestive system. The tannins present in the extract help to reduce inflammation and irritation, while the flavonoids promote relaxation and ease cramps. Additionally, the Maidenhair tree's ability to absorb and eliminate toxins from the body can also contribute to its beneficial effects on stomach discomfort, allowing for a sense of calm and relief from digestive issues.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Saponins, a type of bioactive compound found in maidenhair tree tincture, have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help soothe digestive issues such as cramps, diarrhea, and bloating.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in maidenhair tree tincture have antioxidant properties that can protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and irritation associated with stomach discomfort.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in maidenhair tree tincture exhibit antibacterial and antifungal activities, which may help combat gut imbalances or infections that contribute to stomach discomfort, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make tinctures due to their ability to calm digestive issues and soothe stomach upset, making them an effective treatment for indigestion and bloating.
Barks: The bark is used to create tinctures that can help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) such as abdominal pain, cramping, and diarrhea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a reputable source weigh 100g of the leaves.
Chop the leaves into small pieces and combine with 500ml of vodka or high-proof grain alcohol.
Store the mixture in a dark glass container and allow it to steep for 14 days at room temperature.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discard the solids.
Label the tincture with the date of preparation dosage instructions and storage guidelines store it in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with stomach discomfort because of its natural ability to soothe and calm digestive issues. The active compounds in fennel, such as anethole and flavonoids, work to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the stomach, easing symptoms like bloating, cramps, and diarrhea. Additionally, fennel tinctures help stimulate digestion and relieve gas, leading to a sense of comfort and relief for individuals experiencing stomach upset. This natural remedy is a gentle yet effective way to promote digestive health and alleviate discomfort.
remedy_properties: Carminative properties: Fennel tincture helps to alleviate flatulence, bloating, and cramps by releasing trapped gas in the digestive tract, allowing for easier passage and relief from discomfort.
Antispasmodic properties: Fennel's antispasmodic compounds help to relax the muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can cause stomach pain and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other forms of stomach discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are used to make tinctures due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate stomach cramps and digestive issues.
Seeds: Fennel seeds are a common ingredient in tinctures as they contain natural oils that can help reduce bloating and gas, alleviating discomfort and promoting digestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare aerial parts during full moon for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested material in a warm dark place or use a dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius.
Chop the dried foeniculum vulgare into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar to create a ratio of one part herb to four parts solvent.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction and decarboxylation of active compounds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and label it with preparation date ingredients ratio and dosage instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with stomach discomfort because of its soothing and protective properties. The mucilages in marshmallow root create a coating on the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by acid reflux, digestive disorders, and other gut issues. This coating also helps to neutralize digestive acids and relieve heartburn symptoms. Additionally, marshmallow tinctures can help to relax and calm the stomach muscles, easing spasms and cramps associated with stomach discomfort.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow tincture contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tincture also contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and alleviate symptoms of stomach discomfort such as bloating and cramps.

Demulcent properties: Marshmallow tincture has demulcent properties, meaning it forms a protective film on the surface of the mucous membranes, helping to calm and soothe irritated tissues, and reducing the perception of pain or discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps soothe and protect the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Leaves: Contain a high concentration of flavonoids, antioxidants that help calm digestive issues and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality marshmallow root althaea officinalis in the spring or fall when plants are mature and dry.
Cleanly chop 200 grams of dried marshmallow root into small pieces for optimal extraction.
Combine chopped root with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka at a ratio of 1:5, then stir thoroughly to mix.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking daily to ensure efficient extraction of medicinal properties.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids and reserving the tincture for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with stomach discomfort because it contains apigenin, an antioxidant that soothes the digestive tract and reduces inflammation. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties help to calm cramps, bloating, and gas, while its calming effects on the nervous system ease anxiety and stress-related digestive issues. Additionally, chamomile's natural antimicrobial properties help to eliminate harmful bacteria and yeast that can contribute to stomach discomfort, promoting a healthy gut environment.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which can alleviate symptoms of gastritis, ulcers, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage digestive tissues and contribute to stomach discomfort, promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of chamomile contain a higher concentration of apigenin, a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing stomach discomfort.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers are rich in essential oils and flavonoids, which help to calm digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the gut.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Combine one part of the harvested material with four parts of high-proof vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks shaking the jar every two days.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Store the final tincture in an amber glass bottle at room temperature away from direct sunlight and use within 24 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm tinctures helps with stomach discomfort because it soothes and protects the mucous membranes lining the digestive tract. The tannins in slippery elm create a protective barrier that cushions and calms irritated tissues, reducing inflammation and discomfort. This natural anti-inflammatory action also helps to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), such as cramps, bloating, and diarrhea. remedy_properties: Mucilages: Slippery elm tincture contains mucilages, which are gel-like substances that form a protective barrier on the digestive tract, soothing and calming inflammation, irritation, and ulcers in the stomach lining.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in slippery elm tincture help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the digestive system, which can contribute to stomach discomfort, pain, and acidity.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Slippery elm tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds that inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, reducing inflammation and swelling in the stomach and intestines, which can alleviate symptoms like cramping, bloating, and discomfort.

Barks: Contains a high concentration of slippery elm's characteristic mucilages, making it an effective ingredient for stomach discomfort remedies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ulmus rubra leaves and stems according to sustainable foraging practices, selecting tender young growth.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material using paper towels or clean cloths, gently shaking off excess moisture.
Combine 200g of dried ulmus rubra herb with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a glass jar, ensuring the herb is completely submerged.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks, allowing the mixture to macerate and steep under its own weight.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids and reserving the resulting liquid extract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Konjac tinctures helps with stomach discomfort because of its natural soothing properties that calm digestive issues. The konjac plant's gel-like fiber works to regulate bowel movements, reducing inflammation and cramping in the abdominal area. As it absorbs excess fluids and toxins, it also helps to neutralize stomach acidity, alleviating symptoms such as bloating and gas. Additionally, the tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and discomfort, providing fast relief from digestive distress.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Konjac tincture helps reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate stomach discomfort caused by conditions such as gastritis, ulcers, or irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
Antioxidant property: The antioxidant compounds in konjac tincture neutralize free radicals that can damage gastric mucosa and cause stomach pain, bloating, and discomfort.

Prokinetic property: Konjac tincture's prokinetic effect helps regulate gut motility, improving the movement of food through the digestive system and reducing symptoms like nausea, vomiting, and abdominal cramping.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and protect the digestive tract from irritation.
Rhizomes: Rhizomes contain a high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh amorphophallus konjac tubers of high quality and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Dry the cleaned tubers in shade or using a dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours before grinding into fine powder.
Combine one part of the ground tuber powder with three parts of 80 proof vodka in a glass jar and seal it tightly.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking it well once daily during preparation time.
Strain the liquid tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids after completion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with stomach discomfort because of its natural soothing properties. The herb, also known as Passionflower, contains flavonoids and alkaloids that have a calming effect on the digestive system. When taken in tincture form, Maypop can help to reduce inflammation and ease cramps, bloating, and other symptoms associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other stomach issues. Its anti-inflammatory properties also help to neutralize acid reflux and soothe the esophagus, promoting a sense of comfort and relief.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Maypop contains compounds like scopoletin and umbelliferone, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, helping to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other stomach discomforts.
Antispasmodic: The tannins present in Maypop have a calming effect on the smooth muscle lining the gut, relaxing spasms and cramps that can contribute to stomach pain, bloating, and discomfort.

Antioxidant: Maypop's antioxidant properties help protect the digestive tract from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate conditions like gastritis, ulcers, and inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are used to create a soothing and calming tincture that can help alleviate digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the stomach.
Rhyzomes: The underground stems of the Maypop plant contain anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for creating a tincture that can help reduce stomach pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried passiflora incarnata flowers in the morning for optimal potency and quality.
Weigh out 500g of harvested passiflora incarnata flowers and mix with 1000ml of 95% vodka in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and retaining 350ml of liquid.
Label and store the passiflora incarnata tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years, using as needed with a recommended dosage of 20-30 drops per serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover tinctures helps with stomach discomfort because it contains isoflavones, which have a soothing effect on the digestive system. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea. Additionally, red clover tincture's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to calm stomach ulcers and acid reflux. By promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort, red clover tinctures can bring relief to individuals suffering from stomach upset, allowing them to enjoy a more comfortable digestive experience.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover tincture contains isoflavones, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve digestion, helping to alleviate symptoms of stomach discomfort such as bloating and cramps.

Phytoestrogens: The phytoestrogens present in red clover tincture can help regulate the balance of estrogen levels in the body, which can contribute to stomach discomfort and digestive issues, particularly in women with hormonal imbalances.

Flavonoids: Red clover tincture is rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the red clover plant have a soothing and calming effect on the digestive system, making them an effective part for treating stomach discomfort.
Buds: Red clover buds contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried red clover flowers at dawn when they are most potent.
Weigh 100g of the harvested material and mix it with 400ml of vodka or grain alcohol.
Store the mixture in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid for 4 weeks, shaking daily to extract compounds.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids after 30 minutes of pressing.
Transfer the filtered tincture to dark glass bottles and store it in the refrigerator at 2-3°c for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena tinctures helps with stomach discomfort because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the digestive system. The herb's gentle anti-inflammatory properties ease digestive issues such as bloating, cramps, and gas, while its carminative effects help to expel trapped air and alleviate feelings of heaviness. Anti-inflammatory property: Lemon verbena's anti-inflammatory compound, citral, reduces inflammation in the stomach lining, which can help alleviate symptoms of gastritis, ulcers, and other inflammatory conditions causing stomach discomfort.

Carminative property: Lemon verbena's carminative effect helps to reduce gas and bloating by increasing digestive enzymes and improving gut motility, relieving symptoms of indigestion, flatulence, and dyspepsia.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a common choice for making medicinal tinctures due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, which can help soothe and calm digestive issues.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and stems at dawn when essential oils are most concentrated.
Dry the harvested material in a warm dark place for at least three weeks to preserve potency.
Chop 25 grams of dried verbena officinalis into small pieces and combine with 250 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure proper extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container, discarding solids and transferring the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles for storage.

remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves at dawn or before noon when the essential oils are most concentrated.
Rinse fresh melissa officinalis leaves with cold running water and pat dry with paper towels.
Combine one part dried melissa officinalis leaves with one part 190-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for at least four weeks in a cool dark place, shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store in the refrigerator.

Flowers: The flowers of the pot marigold plant contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that soothe and protect the mucous membranes, providing relief from stomach irritations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula officinalis flowers for their vibrant yellow petals.
Combine one part harvested calendula material with two parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking daily, to extract bioactive compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids and preserving the tincture.
Transfer the calendula officinalis tincture to a dark glass bottle with dropper lid, labeling it for future use.

remedy_parts: Fruits: Rich in fatty acids, flavonoids, and phytosterols, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe digestive issues.
Seeds: Contain a unique combination of fatty acids and sterols that have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve symptoms of stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried serenoa repens leaves in sufficient quantity for desired yield.
Cleanse the harvested material by removing any debris or contaminants thoroughly.
Chop the cleaned leaves into small pieces to increase surface area exposure.
Soak the chopped leaves in high-proof grain alcohol 25% v/v solution 4-6 weeks.
Strain and filter the liquid using cheesecloth or coffee filters twice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw tinctures helps with stomach discomfort because its unique blend of bioactive compounds, including harpagosides and flavonoids, has a natural soothing effect on the digestive system. The herb has been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and pain in the muscles and joints, which can often radiate to the stomach area, causing discomfort. By calming the digestive tract and reducing muscle spasms, devil's claw tinctures provide relief from symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and nausea, promoting a sense of ease and comfort in the stomach region.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Devil's Claw contains anti-inflammatory compounds like harpagosides and glycosides, which help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), acid reflux, and other stomach discomforts.

Stomach soothing properties: The tannins present in Devil's Claw have a protective effect on the stomach mucosa, helping to soothe and calm irritations, reducing inflammation, and preventing further damage from digestive acids and enzymes.

Carminative and antispasmodic effects: The volatile oils and flavonoids in Devil's Claw tinctures exhibit carminative properties, which help relieve bloating, gas, and cramps by relaxing the muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, while also displaying antispasmodic effects that ease stomach contractions and reduce spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in harpagosides, a key compound responsible for the plant's anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in soothing stomach discomfort.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the devil's claw plant contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including sesquiterpenes and triterpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, providing relief for stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried harpagophytum procumbens roots at dawn on a sunny day for optimal potency.
Combine the harvested roots with 50% vodka and cover them in a glass jar leaving no air gaps.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow for solvent extraction and maceration.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container discarding the solids after 30 minutes.
Transfer the resulting tincture to amber glass bottles using dropper caps filling each bottle to 1/3 capacity within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock tinctures helps with stomach discomfort because of its unique ability to soothe and calm digestive issues. The herb's saponins and inulin content work together to reduce inflammation, ease cramps, and regulate bowel movements, providing relief from bloating, gas, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Additionally, burdock tincture's antibacterial properties help eliminate unwanted toxins and microorganisms that can irritate the stomach lining, promoting a healthy gut environment and alleviating symptoms of indigestion and nausea.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Burdock root has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: Burdock's antioxidant properties help protect the gut lining from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of stomach ulcers and other gastrointestinal issues.

Digestive stimulant and normalize bowel function: Burdock tincture helps stimulate digestive enzymes and regulate bowel movements, which can alleviate symptoms such as constipation, diarrhea, and indigestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including indigestion and stomach cramps, due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Buds: Burdock buds are rich in vitamins and minerals, and have been used to soothe stomach discomfort and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh arctium lappa roots and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 6-8 weeks at room temperature shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding solids.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and keep it away from direct sunlight.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat stomach discomfort with herbal tinctures is to start by taking a small dose of Ginger Tincture in water or tea, as its anti-inflammatory properties help soothe the digestive system. Next, try using Peppermint Tincture to ease cramps and reduce inflammation. If symptoms persist, consider adding Dandelion Root Tincture to support liver function and digestion. Always dilute tinctures in water and consult with a healthcare professional before use, especially if you have underlying health conditions or are taking medications.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to stomach discomfort that are treated with herbal tinctures are digestive issues such as bloating, cramps, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Additionally, herbal tinctures may also help alleviate menstrual cramps, anxiety, and insomnia, which can all be exacerbated by poor digestion. Certain herbs like peppermint, ginger, and chamomile have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the digestive tract and promote relaxation.
